_comment: Single source of truth for in-app help and the website support page.
_comment2: platform: 'ios' | 'android' | 'both'. Items filtered at codegen time per platform. screenTip actions may carry their own optional platform (schema 1.10); omitted means both.
_comment3: Consumers MUST ignore unknown top-level keys (forward-compatible additions only).

items.title: What is Wedding Player?
steps: Wedding Player is a wedding day music app designed for the day itself, not for streaming or discovery, but for reliable, offline playback when it matters most.
You build a playlist for each part of your wedding day (called a Moment), queue them in order, then hand the phone to your venue coordinator or a trusted friend.
On the day, one tap starts the music. No Wi-Fi required, no notifications, no surprises.
Wedding Player works with music from your own files, Apple Music, or our built-in collection of original wedding tracks.

items.title: What is a Moment?
steps: A Moment is a named section of your wedding day, for example Walking Down The Aisle, Signing the Register, or Recessional.

Each Moment has its own playlist of music tracks that play in order. The right music plays at exactly the right time, without any manual switching. And when the Moment ends, the music can be faded out with a simple tap, just when it's needed. The next moment is lined up automatically, ready to play with a single tap.

Wedding Player comes with six default Moments: Guests Arriving, Walking Down The Aisle, Signing The Register, The Couple Exit and Wedding Breakfast in the Daytime segment, plus Drinks Reception in the Evening segment. You can rename, reorder, add, or remove them to match your ceremony.
Moments are grouped into Daytime and Evening sections, so the app stays organised for longer celebrations.

items.title: Next Steps
steps: Follow the detailed instructions below to start personalising your moments and tracks to fit your day.

items.title: How do I set up my wedding day moments?
steps: Tap the "Edit Moments" button next to Rehearsal Mode to open the Edit Moments screen.
You'll see the current list of moments organised into Daytime and Evening Sections. From this screen, you can delete a moment by tapping the trash icon at the end of the row, or drag to re-order.
To add a new moment, tap the "Add Moment" button.
Tap any moment to open it to rename it, or to move it to Daytime or Evening Segment and tap "Save Changes"

items.title: How do I add music to a moment?
steps: Tap any moment, then tap "Add Tracks" (on an empty moment) or "Edit Tracks" (once it has any).
Then choose Add Local Files to import existing files from the Files app (MP3, M4A, WAV, AIFF).
Or choose Add Originals to browse Wedding Player's built-in original tracks.
Or choose Add Apple Music to search and add Apple Music tracks (an active Apple Music subscription is required).
Tracks play in order. Drag to reorder them within a moment.

items.title: How do I add music to a moment?
steps: Tap any moment, then tap Edit.
Tap + Edit Tracks to import MP3, M4A, or WAV files from your device storage.
Or choose Originals to browse Wedding Player's built-in original tracks.
Tracks play in order. Drag to reorder them within a moment.

items.title: How do I get music files onto my iPhone or iPad?
steps: Wedding Player plays MP3, M4A, WAV, and AIFF files stored on your device. Three easy ways to get them there:
iCloud Drive: Drop your music files into iCloud Drive on your Mac. They'll appear in the Files app on your iPhone or iPad and in Wedding Player's file picker.
AirDrop: Right-click a file on your Mac and AirDrop it to your phone. iOS will offer to save it to Files.
Mac Finder: Connect your iPhone or iPad to your Mac with a cable, open Finder, select your device in the sidebar, then drag music files into the Files section.
Tip: keep everything in a dedicated 'Wedding Music' folder in Files so it's easy to find on the day.

items.title: How do I get music files onto my Android device?
steps: Wedding Player plays MP3, M4A, WAV, and AIFF files stored on your phone. Three easy ways to get them there:
Google Drive: On your computer, go to drive.google.com and upload your files. In Wedding Player's file picker, tap the menu icon (≡) and choose Google Drive, and your files will appear there.

USB cable: Connect your phone to your computer with a USB cable. Swipe down on your phone and tap the USB notification, then choose File Transfer. Your phone appears as a drive on the computer; drag music into Music or Downloads.
Files by Google: Install Files by Google from the Play Store (free). Use it to move files from Downloads into a Music folder, or to open files directly from cloud services.
Tip: keep everything in a dedicated 'Wedding Music' folder so it's easy to find on the day.

items.title: How do I switch to Live Mode on the day?
steps: Tap Go Live when you're ready to hand over control.
Wedding Player runs a pre-flight check first, flagging any missing tracks or offline issues before switching to the large-control interface.
Fix any warnings before the wedding. Don't dismiss them and hope for the best.
We recommend running through this during rehearsal so there are no surprises on the day.

items.title: How do I make Apple Music tracks available offline?
steps: Open the Offline Readiness checklist in the Settings page.
Any tracks not yet downloaded will show a warning.

Open the Apple Music app and access the Wedding Player Downloads playlist. This playlist is created automatically for you and contains all of the tracks you have added to Wedding Player. Tap the download arrow in the header of the playlist.
Return to Wedding Player. Each track should show a gold tick.
Test playback in Airplane Mode the day before, not on the morning of the wedding.

items.title: Why isn't Spotify supported?
steps: No wedding app can play Spotify, ours included. Spotify's rules stop every app from fading or blending its songs, from playing them offline, and from using them commercially. Even the big DJ platforms only get a stream-only version that stops working if the venue Wi-Fi drops.

Apple Music is different: Apple's MusicKit framework gives apps like Wedding Player full-track playback for subscribers, including offline downloads, real crossfades, and precise cues. That's what Wedding Player uses on iPhone and iPad.
If your music lives in Spotify, find each track on Apple Music (a one-month free trial covers the wedding) and add it through Add Apple Music inside Wedding Player.
Or buy the tracks you need outright (iTunes Store, Amazon Music, Qobuz, Bandcamp) and import them with Add Local Files. On Android this is the main route, alongside Wedding Player Originals.
The full story is at weddingplayer.app/music/spotify

items.title: How do I trim a track or add fade-in and fade-out?
steps: In rehearsal mode, tap Edit Tracks on the moment, then tap the scissors icon on any track row.
Drag the IN-POINT slider to set where the track should start playing, and the OUT-POINT slider to set where it should stop. The Duration field shows the length of the trimmed window.
Fade in at in-point ramps from silence to full volume over the duration set in Settings → Playback (default 5 seconds). Fade out at out-point does the reverse before the out-point.

Use From in-point and To out-point to spot-check the first or last 10 seconds, or Full trimmed clip to hear the whole edit with both fades applied. The countdown inside each button shows time remaining.
Tap Reset Trim at the bottom to clear everything and start over. Tap Save when you're happy. The original file or Apple Music track is unaffected: Wedding Player only stores your trim points.

items.title: Where do I find more music?
steps: Tap Add or Edit Tracks on a moment and tap Get more music at the bottom of the screen.

The tip card lists three on-device routes: buying individual tracks outright (iTunes Store, Bandcamp, Qobuz, 7digital, Amazon Music), free music libraries (Pixabay, Free Music Archive, ccMixter, Incompetech), and adding files you already own from iCloud Drive, Files, AirDrop, or an email attachment.

items.title: Do I need a music licence?
steps: Wedding Player is a player. It helps you organise, time, and reliably play the music you choose. It does not provide music rights or licences, and the app itself does not need a PRS licence.

Performance licences (PRS and PPL): playing recorded music in public can need a licence, known in the UK as TheMusicLicence. This is normally the venue's responsibility, and most licensed venues already hold it. Genuinely private, invitation-only ceremonies are generally not charged. If you are unsure, ask your venue.

Streaming services: if you play from a service like Apple Music, that subscription has its own terms, and those terms are for personal, non-commercial use. That is an agreement between you and the service, separate from any venue licence.

For total peace of mind, especially if you are a celebrant, planner, or venue, build your ceremony from music you own or have purchased, or from our royalty-cleared Wedding Player Originals. For those tracks there is nothing extra to clear on the recording side.

items.title: What does the Ready to Go Live check do?
steps: When you tap Go Live, Wedding Player runs its Ready to Go Live checks before switching to the large-button operator interface. It flags anything that could cause a silent failure on the day, then asks you to confirm a short operator checklist.

Warnings are tiered. Critical items (burgundy) cover issues that will likely break playback: an Apple Music track not downloaded for offline use, Apple Music access not granted, a local file missing from the device, or a segment with no playable moments. Worth-checking items (lighter shade) are informational: a moment with no tracks, or a battery below 30% and not charging.

The operator checklist asks you to confirm Airplane Mode (cellular devices only), Do Not Disturb, that your audio system is connected and tested, and battery if it's low. The Airplane row reads your device's state automatically and shows a Yes or No pill: there's nothing to tap. Do Not Disturb is a manual check: switch it on in Control Centre first, then tap the row to confirm.

If any Apple Music tracks aren't downloaded, an Open Apple Music button drops you straight on the Wedding Player Downloads playlist's download arrow. Once you return to Wedding Player, the readiness sheet refreshes automatically.
Go Live activates only after every checklist row is satisfied. The button turns gold and a subtle scale-up confirms it's ready. One tap hands you to Live Mode.

items.title: What is the airplane icon in Live Mode?
steps: The Airplane icon shows whether Airplane Mode is on. When it's red, Airplane Mode is off and calls, texts, or system alerts could interrupt the music.
Tap the icon to open Settings, then switch Airplane Mode on yourself (iOS no longer allows apps to deep-link to that toggle). The indicator turns green once it's active.
Enable Airplane Mode before every event, ideally as part of the Ready to Go Live checks.

items.title: Can someone else control the music on the day?
steps: Yes. Live Mode is designed exactly for this. Hand your device to your venue coordinator or a trusted friend once Live Mode is active.
The large-button interface is straightforward to use without any knowledge of how the app was set up.
Brief them beforehand on which button starts each moment, and run a rehearsal so they're confident.

items.title: How do I prepare for offline use on the day?
steps: All local audio files work offline automatically. No action needed.
For Apple Music tracks (iOS): open the Offline Readiness page in Settings and follow the instructions there to download every track and verify it's ready for offline playback.
Enable Airplane Mode on the day to block calls and notifications.
Charge your device fully the night before. Keep the app in the foreground and don't close it between moments.

items.title: No sound is playing
steps: Check your device's physical volume buttons.
Make sure the mute switch on the side of the device is off.
If using Bluetooth or an external speaker, confirm it's connected and selected as the audio output.

items.title: A track won't play or shows as missing
steps: For local files: the file may have been moved or deleted from the device. Remove it from the moment and re-import it.
For Apple Music tracks: check your subscription is active and the track is downloaded for offline use (see Offline Readiness in Settings).
If the file was in iCloud Drive and has been removed from your device, download it in the Files app first.
Try closing and reopening the app if the issue persists.

items.title: A track won't play or shows as missing
steps: The file may have been moved or deleted from your device. Remove it from the moment and re-import it.
Check the file format is supported: MP3, M4A, and WAV files work. FLAC and OGG are not currently supported.
Try closing and reopening the app if the issue persists.

items.title: Volume levels are inconsistent between tracks
steps: Wedding Player normalises volume automatically when you import a local file, bringing everything to a consistent level.
If a track still sounds noticeably louder or quieter, try removing and re-importing it.
On iOS, if Apple Music tracks vary in volume, check that Sound Check is enabled in Settings → Music on your iPhone.

items.title: Volume levels are inconsistent between tracks
steps: Wedding Player normalises volume automatically when you import a local file, bringing everything to a consistent level.
If a track still sounds noticeably louder or quieter, try removing and re-importing it.

items.title: A track has a padlock icon and won't play
steps: A padlock means the file has DRM protection and can't play outside Apple's own apps.
This affects music purchased from the iTunes Store before 2009, when Apple used FairPlay copy protection.
Check your iTunes purchase history. Apple has upgraded many older purchases to DRM-free versions you can re-download at no cost.
Alternatively, if the song is on Apple Music, add it directly inside Wedding Player without any file management.

items.title: A file doesn't appear when I tap Add Files
steps: The Files picker only shows files visible to the iOS Files app.
Make sure your file is in iCloud Drive, On My iPhone, or a connected cloud service like Dropbox or Google Drive (with their iOS apps installed).
If the file shows a cloud icon (not yet downloaded to the device), tap it once in the Files app to download it before importing.

items.title: A file doesn't appear when I tap Add Files
steps: The Files picker only shows files visible on your device.
Make sure your file is in on your device, or a connected cloud service like Dropbox or Google Drive.
If the file shows a cloud icon (not yet downloaded to the device), tap it once in the Files app to download it before importing.

items.title: Restore Purchases isn't working
steps: Make sure you're signed in with the same account used for the original purchase.
Tap Restore Purchases in Settings → Account.
If it still doesn't work, contact support with your original purchase receipt.

actions.label: Set start and end points
actions.context: Drag the gold and burgundy sliders to clip the track to the part you want. Or type exact mm:ss values into the timestamp fields. Trimming does not add a fade on its own: use the two fade switches below if you want one.

actions.label: Fade in and fade out
actions.context: Toggle the fade switches to ramp from silence at the start and fade to silence at the end. Fade duration is set in Settings under Playback.

actions.label: Preview your trim
actions.context: Three preview buttons let you hear From in-point (first 10s), To out-point (last 10s), or the Full trimmed clip end-to-end. For Apple Music tracks the preview uses the main player briefly: the Now Playing card will show the previewed track.

actions.label: Preview your trim
actions.context: Three preview buttons let you hear Start (first 10s), End (last 10s), or the Full trimmed clip end-to-end.

actions.label: Reset trim
actions.context: At the bottom of the editor: clears all trim points and fade settings on this track. Asks for confirmation first.

actions.label: Save your edits
actions.context: Tap Save to apply your changes; Cancel discards them. Trim points and fade settings persist with the track across app launches.

actions.label: Rehearsal Screen
actions.context: This is the main screen where you build your Wedding Day timeline from.

actions.label: First - Edit your moments
actions.context: Each moment is a separate row or block - Tap Edit Moments at the bottom of the screen to add, rename, reorder, or delete the moments of your Wedding Day.

actions.label: Next - Add music to each moment
actions.context: Tap Edit Tracks on each moment to add or delete tracks from your local files, Wedding Player Originals or from Apple Music.

actions.label: Next - Add music to each moment
actions.context: Tap Edit Tracks on each moment to add or delete tracks from your local files or Wedding Player Originals.

actions.label: Play Through
actions.context: Once you've arranged your moments and added music tracks, you can rehearse your full Wedding Day timeline. Tap the big button to play and tap again to fade out. If you have multiple tracks, use the Next Track button to jump to the next track in the moment.

actions.label: What the icons on a track row mean
actions.context: Scissor icon on a track row means the track is trimmed, and the waveform icon means a fade is set: both appear only where you have edited that track. A cloud icon at the start of a row means an Apple Music track has not been downloaded yet, and needs downloading for faultless playback. Tap the cloud icon on the row to open the download checklist. These icons appear while you are building and are hidden in Live Mode.

actions.label: What the icons on a track row mean
actions.context: Scissor icon on a track row means the track is trimmed, and the waveform icon means a fade is set: both appear only where you have edited that track. These icons appear while you are building and are hidden in Live Mode.

actions.label: Switch to Live Mode
actions.context: When you're ready for the day, tap Go Live for a simplifed version of this screen, without the editing controls.

actions.label: Review warnings first
actions.context: Critical items (burgundy) will likely cause silent playback failure: missing tracks, no Apple Music access. Fix these before Live. Worth-checking items are lighter and informational.

actions.label: Review warnings first
actions.context: Critical items (burgundy) will likely cause silent playback failure, such as missing tracks. Fix these before Live. Worth-checking items are lighter and informational.

actions.label: Open Apple Music if needed
actions.context: The Open Apple Music button takes you to the Apple Music app. Navigate to your Playlists and open the Wedding Player Downloads playlist. Download the track(s) and return to Wedding Player: the row refreshes automatically.

actions.label: Confirm each operator check
actions.context: The Airplane row reads your device automatically and shows Yes or No. Do Not Disturb is a manual confirm: toggle it in Control Centre first, then tap the row. The audio and battery rows are tap-to-confirm.

actions.label: Go Live
actions.context: The Go Live button activates once every checklist row is satisfied: it turns gold and lifts slightly. One tap hands you over to Live Mode.

screenTips.title: Running the music on the day

actions.label: Play the current moment
actions.context: The big play button starts the music for whatever moment is showing. One tap: hand the phone over and trust it.

actions.label: Check the airplane indicator
actions.context: Top of the screen shows whether Airplane Mode is on. Tap to enable: blocks calls and notifications on the day.

actions.label: Move to the next moment
actions.context: When the moment finishes, the big button fades the current track and cues the next moment. Tap once to fade out, then again to start the next.

actions.label: Exit Live Mode
actions.context: Tap the LIVE MODE banner at the top of the screen, then confirm Exit. Useful during rehearsal, not on the day itself.

actions.label: Add a new moment
actions.context: Tap Add Moment to create a section like 'First Dance' or 'Speeches'. Pick whether it sits in the Daytime or Evening segment.

actions.label: Rename or move a moment
actions.context: Tap any moment to open it. Rename it, change its short name (used on tabs), or switch its segment.

actions.label: Reorder your running order
actions.context: Drag moments up or down. The order here is the order Live Mode plays them in.

actions.label: Delete unused moments
actions.context: Tap the trash icon at the end of the row. Live Mode hides empty moments automatically, but deleting keeps your setup clean.

actions.label: Add tracks
actions.context: Use the buttons at the bottom of the screen to add music tracks from those sources

actions.label: Trim and fade
actions.context: Tap a track to open the trim editor. Set custom start and end points, add smooth fade-ins and fade-outs at each end. Tap the ? button inside the editor for more detail.

actions.label: Move track to another moment
actions.context: Tap on the icon to the left of the delete icon to move the track to another moment.

actions.label: Delete a track
actions.context: Tap the trash icon on the right of the row to remove the track from this moment. The original file or Apple Music track is unaffected.

actions.label: Delete a track
actions.context: Tap the trash icon on the right of the row to remove the track from this moment. The original file is unaffected.

actions.label: Reorder tracks
actions.context: If you have multiple tracks in the moment, just drag them into any order using the three line icon on the right of the row,

actions.label: Loop Moment
actions.context: Use the Loop Moment toggle in the moment editor. Once switched on, the moment plays in a loop until you fade and end. With multiple tracks all play in order, then wrap back to the first. A red looping indicator shows during playback.

actions.label: Apple Music Integration
actions.context: Use the tabs above to Search for Tracks, Access Your Playlists and Browse Apple Music Collections - Note an Apple Music Subscription is required.

actions.label: Search the Apple Music catalogue
actions.context: Type a song title, artist, or album. Requires an active Apple Music subscription.

actions.label: Browse your playlists
actions.context: Tap My Playlists to see music you've already added or downloaded in the Music app.

actions.label: Browse
actions.context: Tap Browse to access Apples Prebuilt Wedding Collections

actions.label: Download for offline use
actions.context: After adding tracks, open Offline Readiness in Settings and download the auto-created Wedding Player Downloads playlist via the Music app.

actions.label: Browse Originals
actions.context: Originals are exclusive tracks bundled with Wedding Player. Tap any track to preview, or tap the download arrow to add it to your library.

actions.label: Browse More Originals
actions.context: Tap on Browse More Originals to preview and download further tracks.

actions.label: Multi-select to add several
actions.context: If you have some tracks downloaded, they will appear on the screen. Tap to select the track(s) you want to add. The trailing button label switches to 'Add N tracks' once anything is selected. Tap it to import them all at once into the current moment.

actions.label: Already-used tracks are locked
actions.context: Tracks you've already added to another moment show 'Used in [Moment]' and can't be picked twice. This prevents accidental duplicates in your running order.

actions.label: Preview a track
actions.context: Tap the play circle to hear a 15-second preview. Use the category chips to filter by moment.

actions.label: Download a track
actions.context: Tap the download arrow to save the full track. Once downloaded, it's available offline and the play circle plays the whole track.

actions.label: Add to your moments
actions.context: Open any moment, tap Add Originals, and pick from the tracks you've downloaded. One track can only be used in a single moment.

actions.label: Free up space
actions.context: Tap the red trash icon next to a downloaded track to remove it. You can re-download later from the same screen.

actions.label: Manage moments
actions.context: Edit Moments takes you straight to the Moment Manager: add, rename, reorder, delete.

actions.label: Apple Music setup
actions.context: Connect Apple Music, see subscription status, and check what's downloaded for offline playback.

actions.label: Offline Readiness
actions.context: See which tracks are ready without Wi-Fi. Run this check the day before the wedding.

actions.label: Version + support
actions.context: Your build number, contact details, and the full Help screen all live at the bottom of Settings.

actions.label: Browse by topic
actions.context: Help is grouped into How It Works, Getting Started, On The Day, and Troubleshooting. Tap a question to expand its steps.

actions.label: Looking for something specific?
actions.context: If a topic isn't here, weddingplayer.app has the full guide, and you can email support if you're still stuck.

actions.label: Per-screen tips elsewhere
actions.context: Most screens have a ? button in the corner for a quick tip card. Use those in-flow; come here for the full reference.

actions.label: Apple Music setup steps
actions.context: To ensure all Apple Music tracks will download, follow the two instructions in this section.

actions.label: Check all files are showing as Ready
actions.context: If a file is marked as Ready, it means it has downloaded.

actions.label: Files showing Download
actions.context: Make sure the Apple Music Setup steps are completed. Open Apple Music and navigate to the Wedding Player playlist. Tap the download arrow to download all tracks.

actions.label: Test in Airplane Mode
actions.context: Once everything shows a gold tick, switch on Airplane Mode and play through every moment. Do this the day before, not the morning of.

actions.label: Add couple names
actions.context: Enter the names that show on the player's header, e.g. 'Nicola & Alex'. Visible during rehearsal and on the live banner.

actions.label: Set the wedding date
actions.context: The date drives the post-wedding rating prompt and the personalised header. Change it any time.

actions.label: Other personalisation
actions.context: Custom text size, app appearance (light / dark / system), and audio engine settings live under the main Settings screen.
